content.full_text:
State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission

P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • • • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

Technical Assistance • • • Administration • • • Executive/Legal • • • Enforcement • • • Conflict of Interest
(916) 322 5662        322-5660          322-5901        322-5441        322-6444

December 20, 1979

William A. Gissler, Mayor
Office of the City Attorney
City of Santa Clara
1500 Warburton Avenue
Santa Clara, CA 95050

Dear Mr. Gissler:

Thank you for your letter dated November 8, 1979, seeking
advice pursuant to Government Code Section 83114(b) regarding
the disqualification provision of the Political Reform Act.
The facts, as I understand them, are as follows.

You are Mayor of the City of Santa Clara and are presently
employed by the engineering firm of Ruth & Going, Inc., a
professional service organization that supplies architectural,
planning, land survey, as well as engineering services to
both public and private clients, including apartment complexes
interested in converting to condominiums. Your present
position with Ruth & Going is that of Senior Civil Service
Engineer and your major function is to "interface with potential clients in a business development capacity."

There have been four applications for condominium conversion before the City of Santa Clara - Vista Del Lago, Parkside
Place, Family Tree Apartments and Lawrence Square West.
Ruth & Going has been involved in some capacity with three
of these four applicants. With respect to Vista Del Lago,
Ruth & Going prepared the final map for condominium conversion;
Ruth & Going was the civil engineering consultant on
the Parkside Place conversion project; and Ruth & Going is
under contract to Family Tree Apartments to provide engineering
and design services. Ruth & Going was not retained by
Lawrence Square West to assist it with its condominium conversion application. With regard to Family Tree, the firm has
represented Family Tree Apartments during all phases of its
application for condominium conversion before the City.
Your involvement to date with the Family Tree Project as an
employee of Ruth & Going has consisted of consultations with

A-79-094

William A. Gissler, Mayor
December 20, 1979
Page Two


both the project engineer and owners regarding both the
economic and political viability of the projects. Pending
before the City are two ordinances which would affect condo-
minium conversion in the City of Santa Clara by easing some
of the present requirements that have made condominium con-
version difficult, if not prohibitively expensive.

There are two questions that you have asked our office
to consider. First, you have asked whether you may participate
in proceedings regarding the proposed ordinances. Second,
you have asked whether you must abstain from participating
in decisions specifically relating to the Family Tree condo-
minium conversion application. Answers to these questions
require consideration of the disqualification provision of
the Political Reform Act, Government Code Section 87100,
which provides:

         No public official at any level of state or local
         government shall make, participate in making or in
         any way attempt to use his official position to
         influence a governmental decision in which he
         knows or has reason to know he has a financial
         interest.

Government Code Section 87103 provides:

         An official has a financial interest in a decision
         within the meaning of Section 87100 if it is reason-
         ably foreseeable that the decision will have a
         material financial effect, distinguishable from
         its effect on the public generally, on:

                                . . .

         (c) Any source of income, other than loans by a
         commercial lending institution in the regular
         course of business, aggregating two hundred fifty
         dollars ($250) or more in value received by or
         promised to the public official within twelve
         months prior to the time when the decision is
         made; or

         (d) Any business entity in which the public offi-
         cial is a director, officer, partner, trustee,
         employee, or holds any position of management.

                                . . .

William A. Gissler, Mayor
December 20, 1979
Page Three


     Since you are an employee of and have received in excess
of $250 from Ruth & Going within the last year, you must
refrain from participating in governmental decisions which
could foreseeably have a material financial effect on Ruth &
Going. By regulation, the Commission has defined the kinds
of financial effects which will be considered material within
the meaning of Government Code Section 87103.  See 2 Cal.
Admin. Code Section 18702. One of the guidelines applicable
to business entities is as follows:

     Whether, in the case of a business entity in which
     the public official holds a direct or indirect
     investment of one thousand dollars ($1,000) or
     more or in the case of a business entity in which
     the public official is a director, officer, partner,
     employee, trustee or holds any position of management, the effect of the decision will be to increase
     or decrease:

          (A)  The annualized gross revenues by the
          lesser of:

               1.  One hundred thousand dollars ($100,000);
               or

                    2.  One percent if it is one thousand
               dollars ($1,000) or more.

                         . . .

     The proposed ordinances, if adopted, will allow additional
condominium conversion to take place in Santa Clara. It is
more than likely that Ruth & Going will be retained by some
of these applicants because the company has been actively
involved in all but one of the previous condominium conversions
that have taken place in the city.  Based upon the confidential
financial information you have provided us regarding Ruth &
Going, it would take only a few additional conversions to
generate sufficient revenues to Ruth & Going to meet the
above guidelines.  Government Code Sections 87100 and 87103
therefore require that you refrain from voting on the proposed
zoning ordinances because it is reasonably foreseeable that
the decision will have a material financial effect on Ruth &
Going.

William A. Gissler, Mayor
December 20, 1979
Page Four


With respect to your second question, although you did
not play a lead role with Ruth & Going in developing Family
Tree's application for presentation to the City, you were
nevertheless involved in that process by rendering advice to
the company and the Family Tree owners regarding the methods
for obtaining governmental approval of condominium conversions. There is, therefore, a nexus between the decisions
before the City and your activity on behalf of both & Going.
See 2 Cal. Adm. Code Section 18702(b)(3)(B). Consequently,
you may not participate in governmental decisions concerning
the conversion of Family Tree.

Finally, because your employer, Ruth & Going, was initially involved in the preparation of Family Tree's conversion
application and has represented Family Tree on all matters
pertaining to the conversion before the City, success of the
application would encourage other potential applicants to
retain Ruth & Going to perform similar services on their
behalf. This is another manner in which your participation
could result in significant financial effects on Ruth &
Going. See 2 Cal. Adm. Code Section 18702(a).

I hope that this advice is helpful. If you have any
questions regarding this matter, please do not hesitate to
contact me.

Very truly yours,

Dwight E. Dickerson
Counsel
Legal Division

DD:kp

THE CITY OF SANTA CLARA
CALIFORNIA

CITY ATTORNEY
CITY HALL
1500 WARBURTON AVE.
SANTA CLARA, CA. 95050
(408) 984-3232

November 8, 1979


Fair Political Practices Commission
926 "J" Street, Suite 522
Sacramento, California 95814

Attention: Dwight Dickerson, Esq.

Dear Mr. Dickerson:

I hereby respectfully request written advice from the Fair Political
Practices Commission for guidance in my official activities based on
the following facts which I believe are material.

MATERIAL FACTS

1. Preliminary Overview

I am the elected mayor of the City of Santa Clara. I am employed
by a civil engineering firm which from time to time represents
applicants seeking to construct community ownership projects (town-
houses and condominiums) and to convert apartment complexes into
condominiums. The City of Santa Clara is currently amending its
Zoning Ordinance provisions which regulate both new condominiums and
the conversion of apartments into condominiums. The City is also
making certain amendments to its Fire Prevention Code which pertain
to life safety systems (smoke detectors, sprinklers, roofing material,
etc.) applicable to condominiums (both new and conversions).

2. Political Status

I became a councilman on April 10, 1973. I was elected mayor
and assumed that position on April 12, 1977. I receive $1,000 a
month in compensation from the City for my services as mayor. I
serve a four year term which will expire in 1981.

Dwight Dickerson, Esq.                    -2-                    November 8, 1979


3.  Employment Background

     A.  Employer

          My employer is Ruth & Going, Inc., 919 The Alameda, P.O. Box
26430, San Jose, California 95159.  Ruth & Going, Inc. is a
professional service organization supplying architectural, engineering,
planning, and land survey services to both public and private clients.
Ruth & Going, Inc. employs approximately 70 persons.

     B.  Term of Employment

          I worked as an independent consultant to Ruth & Going, Inc.
from February 5, 1977, to April 16, 1977.  At that time, April 16,
1977, I became a salaried employee of Ruth & Going, Inc.  I remain
an employee at the present time.

     C.  Position in Firm

          I am a Senior Civil Engineer and serve occasionally as project
engineer on the design of various municipal and private improvement
projects.  I have not however served in that capacity on condominium
design or conversion projects.  My major function is to interface with
potential clients in a business development capacity.  The types of
projects and the services provided by my employer are described in
the Ruth & Going, Inc. brochure, a copy of which is attached as
Exhibit "A".

     D.  Salary/Fringe Benefits

          I currently earn approximately $35,000 a year in compensation
from Ruth & Going, Inc., based upon a fixed hourly rate.  My salary
is not affected by the success or failure of my employer's involvement
with a project.  There is also a profit sharing fund in which I am a
participant.  The profit sharing fund is a ten year vesting program;
that is to say, each year the company places a portion of its profits
in my name into the fund.  I become entitled ("vested") to 10% each
year of the cumulative deposited amount up until the tenth year, at
which time I am entitled to a full 100% of the funds vested in my
name.  The amount placed into the profit sharing fund is based on
the amount of the year's profit, if any, proportioned according to a
formula based primarily on the individual participant's gross annual
earnings.  I became enrolled in the program on November 1, 1977.

     E.  Ruth & Going, Inc. Financial Background Information

          The Ruth & Going, Inc. financial background information is
submitted with the request that the information be kept confidential

Dwight Dickerson, Esq.                    -3-                    November 8, 1979


and be used only by the Fair Political Practices Commission (herein-
after "Commission").  The information reflects rounded off figures.

     The financial background information is attached hereto as
Exhibit "B".

4.  Ruth & Going, Inc.'s Role in Condominium Conversions

     There have been four requests for condominium conversions submitted
to the City.  The role of Ruth & Going, Inc. is indicated as follows:

          1.  Vista Del Lago

               Ruth & Going, Inc. prepared only the final map
               for the condominium conversion.

          2.  Parkside Place

               Ruth & Going, Inc. was the civil engineering
               consultant on the project.

          3.  Family Tree Apartments (instant matter)

               Ruth & Going, Inc. is the civil engineer for
               the conversion of Family Tree Apartments into
               condominiums.  For their services (which
               includes the use of outside consultants) the
               firm has been paid in excess of $25,000 on
               the Family Tree project.  My involvement with
               the Family Tree project has been limited to
               business development activities at the outset.
               In addition, there were some subsequent minor
               consultations with the project engineer and
               the owners regarding procedural matters and
               the relaying of information on City Council
               actions regarding the Family Tree application.

          4.  Lawrence Square West

               The engineering firm of Creegan & D'Angelo is
               the civil engineering consultant on the project.

5.  Current Matters Pending Before the Santa Clara City Council

     The following two matters, for which I would like a determination
of my ability to participate, are pending before the City Council.

Dwight Dickerson, Esq.                    -4-                    November 8, 1979


     A.  Condominium Conversion Ordinances

          Two ordinances are currently pending before the City Council.
The first (Ordinance No. 1390) is a revision of provisions of
Article 27 (Planned Development - PD Zoning District) of "The
Zoning Ordinance of the City of Santa Clara, California".  Article 27
includes community ownership projects which, in turn, includes
condominiums (new and conversions).  The second (Ordinance No. 1391)
makes certain additions to Article 14 of the Fire Prevention Code
which pertain specifically to life safety systems applicable to
condominiums (new and conversions).  Both ordinances make certain
impositions on condominiums (new and conversions) to comply with
fire safety standards.

          The following exhibits are attached hereto:

          Exhibit "C" -  Ordinance 1390 (amending Zoning Ordinance
                         Article 27)
                         Special attention should be directed to
                         Sections 27-8 and 27-9.

          Exhibit "D" -  Ordinance 1391 (additions to Article 14 of
                         the Fire Prevention Code)

          Exhibit "E" -  Existing Article 27 of the Zoning Ordinance

          Exhibit "F" -  Existing Article 14 of the Fire Prevention
                         Code

     B.  The Family Tree Applications

          Also pending before the City Council is the application of
my employer's client, The Family Tree project, to convert 120 apart-
ments into condominiums.  In connection with the application for
conversion, City Council action is required to approve a subdivision
map, approve a zoning change (from apartment zoning to PD zoning)
and grant a variance for parking and for two-hour fire wall protection.

6.  Subjective Statement

     Since 1973 I have been interested in the issue of apartment to
condominium conversion.  I have met with builders, building officials,
attorneys, title company representatives, apartment owners, tenants,
realtors, legislators, and others to learn their viewpoint on the
subject.  I did this because I had the education and the background
to intelligently evaluate the pros and cons of the issue.  I did it
because I felt that the healthy preservation of our city's housing
stock is and will continue to be an important issue in our community,

Dwight Dickerson, Esq.                    -5-                    November 8, 1979


an issue on which I should be able to speak and vote because it
significantly affects the future of our city.  When my viewpoints,
which were based on these extensive studies, conflicted with the
viewpoints of certain political adversaries, I was challenged. To
take the easy way out, I simply abstained from voting on the issue.
I think now, as I did then, that this was an injustice. I did not
contest it because I did not want to subject myself and my family to
the publicity of the appeals process. Now, however, since another
councilmember has been challenged on the issue and an extension of
time has been approved by the Council, I feel it is appropriate to
make this written request.

                            REQUESTED OPINION

In accordance with Government Code § 83114(b), as implemented by your
regulation contained in 2 Cal.Admin. Code § 18329, I respectfully
request that the Fair Political Practices Commission provide written
advice with respect to my Conflict of Interest duties in connection
with City Council consideration of proposals for:

     1.  My voting on the proposed zoning ordinances
         changes effecting condominiums (new and
         conversions) and amendments to the Fire
         Prevention Code which pertain to
         condominiums (new and conversions).

     2.  The application of Family Tree for conversion
         from an apartment complex into a condominium
         project.

Please forward any materials, correspondence, requests for additional
information to the Office of the City Attorney, City of Santa Clara,
1500 Warburton Avenue, Santa Clara, California 95050. If it is
convenient for you to do so, you may contact the City Attorney's
Office by telephone (408) 984-3232. Michael R. Downey, Assistant
City Attorney, is most familiar with this matter.

                                            Very truly yours,


                                            [signature]
                                            WILLIAM A. GISSLER

bd

enclosures

William A. Gissler, Mayor
Office of the City Attorney
City of Santa Clara
1500 Warburton Avenue
Santa Clara, CA 95050

A-99-094

Dear Mr. Gissler:

     Thank you for your letter dated November 8, 1979, seeking
advice pursuant to Government Code Section 83114(b) regarding
the disqualification provision of the Political Reform Act.

     The facts, as I understand them, are as follows.


     You are Mayor of the City of Santa Clara and presently
employed by the engineering firm of Ruth & Going, Inc, Ruth
& Going, Inc., is a professional service organization that
supplies architectural, planning, land survey, as well as
engineering services to both public and private clients.
Part of Ruth & Going, Inc.'s services include condominium
conversion.  Your present position with Ruth & Going, Inc.,
is that of Senior Civil Service Engineer.  Your major function
is to "interface with potential clients in a business development
capacity."


     There have been four applications for condominium conversion
before the City, Vista Del Lago, Parkside Place, Family
Tree Apartments and Lawrence Square West.  Ruth & Going,
Inc., has been involved in some capacity with three of the
four applicants.  With respect to Vista Del Lago, Ruth &
Going, Inc., prepared the final map for condominium.  Ruth &
Going, Inc., was the civil engineering consultant on the
Parkside Place conversion project.  Ruth & Going, Inc., is

Page 2

under contract to Family Tree Apartments to provide engineering

and other design services, for the purposes of condominium

conversion in the City of Santa Clara.  The firm has represented

Family Tree Apartments during all phases of its application

for condominium conversion before the City.  Your involvement

to date with the Family Tree Project as an employee of Ruth

& Going, Inc., has been minor consultations with the project

engineer and owners.


There is pending before the City two ordinances which

would affect condominium conversion in the City of Santa Clara.

The ordinance; would ease some of the present requirements

regarding community ownership projects that had made condominium

conversion difficult, if not prohibitive.


There is two questions that you have asked our office

to consider.  First, may you participate in those proceedings

regarding the proposed ordinances affecting condominium

conversion?  Second, must you abstain from participating in

those decisions by the City that affect the Family Tree

condominium conversion application?  Government Code Section

87100 provides:


     No public official at any level of state or local

     government shall make, participate in making or in

     any way attempt to use his official position to

     influence a governmental decision in which he

     knows or has reason to know he has a financial

     interest.

Page 3


Government Code Section 87103(6) provides:

(c) Any source of income, other than loans by a commercial

lending institution in the regular course of business,

aggregating two hundred fifty dollars ($250) or

more in value received by or promised to the public

official within twelve months prior to the time

when the decision is made.


You have received in excess of $250 from Ruth & Going,

Inc., within the last year.  Therefore, you must refrain

from participating in those decisions which could materially

affect Ruth & Going, Inc.


The Commission has provided guidance through regulation

for determining a material financial affect.  Commission

Regulation 18702 provides:


(a)  The financial effect of a governmental decision

on a financial interest of a public official is

material if the decision will have a significant

effect on the business entity, real property or

source of income in question.


(b)  In determining whether it is reasonably foreseeable

that the effects of a governmental decision will

be significant within the meaning of the general

p. 9

standard set forth in paragraph (a), consideration

should be given to the following factors:

. . .

(3) Whether, in the case of a source of

income as defined in Government Code Section

87103(c), of two hundred fifty dollars ($250)

or more received by or promised to a public

official within 12 months prior to the time

the decision is made:

(A) The effect of the decision will be

to directly increase or decrease the

amount of income (other than rents) to

be received by the official by one hundred

dollars ($100) or more; or

(B) There is a nexus between the governmental

decision and the purpose for which the

official receives income; or

(C) In the case of a source of income

which is a business entity, the business

entity will be affected in a manner

described in subsection (b)(1) above; or

Page 5

(D) If the source of income is not a

business entity, the decision will have

a significant effect on the source.

The proposed ordinances would allow additional condominium

conversion to take place in Santa Clara. It is highly probable

that Ruth & Going, Inc., will capture some of the conversion

work, and that this additional amount of work will, at a

minimum, increase Ruth & Going, Inc.'s annual gross revenues

by $24,000; or its annual net income by $1,000; or its current

assets or liabilities by $12,000. These affects are material

within the meaning of Commission Regulation 18702(a) and

(b)(3)(C). Therefore, you are required to refrain, pursuant

to Government Code Sections 87100 and 87103(c), from voting

on the proposed zoning ordinances affecting condominium

conversion.

These provisions also prohibit you from participating

in those governmental decisions regarding the Family Tree

Apartments' application for condominium conversion because

Ruth & Going, Inc., will be significantly affected by the

governmental decisions, regarding Family Tree, as well as the

existence of a nexus between the decision and the purpose

for which you receive income. Your employer, Ruth & Going,

Inc., was initially involved in the preparation of Family

Tree's application, and the company has represented Family

Tree on all matters pertaining to the conversion before the

Page 6

City. Success of the application would encourage other

potential applicants to retain Ruth & Going, Inc., to perform

similar services on their behalf. Such a potential affect

is significant and material within the meaning of Commission

Regulation 18702(a). Therefore, you must refrain from participating

in governmental decisions affecting the Family Tree application.

[handwritten: Operative basis is also barred because /]

There exists a nexus between the governmental decisions

regarding Family Tree and the purposes for which you receive

income from Ruth & Going, Inc. Although you did not play a

lead role with the company in developing Family Tree's application

for presentation to the City, you were nevertheless involved

in that process by rendering advice to Ruth & Going, Inc.,

and the Family Tree owners regarding condominium conversion.

Such a nexus between the decisions before the counsel and

your activity on behalf of Ruth & Going, Inc., constitutes a

material financial affect on your employer within the meaning

of Commission Regulation 18702(b)(3)(B). Therefore, Government

Code Section 87100 and 87103 prohibits you from participating

in those City matters pertaining to the Family Tree condominium

conversion application.


I hope that this advice is helpful. If you have any

questions regarding this matter, please do not hesitate to

contact me.

[handwritten: Sincerely,]

Very truly yours,


Dwight E. Dickerson
Counsel
Legal Division

THE CITY OF SANTA CLARA
CALIFORNIA

November 8, 1979


Fair Political Practices Commission
926 "J" Street, Suite 522
Sacramento, California 95814

Attention: Dwight Dickerson, Esq.

I hereby respectfully request written advice from the Fair Political

     3.  Employment Background

          A.  Employer

               My employer is Ruth & Going, Inc., 919 The Alameda, P.O. Box
          26430, San Jose, California 95159.  Ruth & Going, Inc. is a
          professional service organization supplying architectural, engineering,
          planning, and land survey services to both public and private clients.
          Ruth & Going, Inc. employs approximately 70 persons.

               I worked as an independent consultant to Ruth & Going, Inc.
          from February 5, 1977, to April 16, 1977.  At that time, April 16,
          1977, I became a salaried employee of Ruth & Going, Inc.  I remain
          an employee at the present time.

          C.  Position in Firm

               I am a Senior Civil Engineer and serve occasionally as project
          engineer on the design of various municipal and private improvement
          projects.  I have not however served in that capacity on condominium

          the Ruth & Going, Inc. brochure, a copy of which is attached as
          Exhibit "A".

          D.  Salary/Fringe Benefits

               I currently earn approximately $35,000 a year in compensation
          from Ruth & Going, Inc., based upon a fixed hourly rate.  My salary
          is not affected by the success or failure of my employer's involvement
          with a project.  There is also a profit sharing fund in which I am a
          participant.  The profit sharing fund is a ten year vesting program;
          that is to say, each year the company places a portion of its profits
          in my name into the fund.  I become entitled ("vested") to 10% each
          year of the cumulative deposited amount up until the tenth year, at
          which time I am entitled to a full 100% of the funds vested in my
          name.  The amount placed into the profit sharing fund is based on

          formula based primarily on the individual participant's gross annual
          earnings.  I became enrolled in the program on November 1, 1977.

          E.  Ruth & Going, Inc. Financial Background Information

               The Ruth & Going, Inc. financial background information is
          submitted with the request that the information be kept confidential

Dwight Dickerson, Esq.                    -3-                    November 8, 1979


and be used only by the Fair Political Practices Commission (herein-
after "Commission"). The information reflects rounded off figures.

     The financial background information is attached hereto as
Exhibit "B".

4. Ruth & Going, Inc.'s Role in Condominium Conversions

     There have been four requests for condominium conversions submitted
to the City. The role of Ruth & Going, Inc. is indicated as follows:

     1. Vista Del Lago

        Ruth & Going, Inc. prepared only the final map
        for the condominium conversion.

     2. Parkside Place

        Ruth & Going, Inc. was the civil engineering
        consultant on the project.

     3. Family Tree Apartments (instant matter)

        Ruth & Going, Inc. is the civil engineer for
        the conversion of Family Tree Apartments into
        condominiums. For their services (which
        includes the use of outside consultants) the
        firm has been paid in excess of $25,000 on
        the Family Tree project. My involvement with
        the Family Tree project has been limited to
        business development activities at the outset.
        In addition, there were some subsequent minor
        consultations with the project engineer and
        the owners regarding procedural matters and
        the relaying of information on City Council
        actions regarding the Family Tree application.

     4. Lawrence Square West

        The engineering firm of Creegan & D'Angelo is
        the civil engineering consultant on the project.

5. Current Matters Pending Before the Santa Clara City Council

     The following two matters, for which I would like a determination
of my ability to participate, are pending before the City Council.

Dwight Dickerson, Esq.                    -4-                    November 8, 1979


A.  Condominium Conversion Ordinances

     Two ordinances are currently pending before the City Council.
The first (Ordinance No. 1390) is a revision of provisions of
Article 27 (Planned Development - PD Zoning District) of "The
Zoning Ordinance of the City of Santa Clara, California". Article 27
includes community ownership projects which, in turn, includes

makes certain additions to Article 14 of the Fire Prevention Code
which pertain specifically to life safety systems applicable to
condominiums (new and conversions). Both ordinances make certain
impositions on condominiums (new and conversions) to comply with
fire safety standards.

     The following exhibits are attached hereto:

     Exhibit "C" -  Ordinance 1390 (amending Zoning Ordinance
                    Article 27)
                    Special attention should be directed to
                    Sections 27-8 and 27-9.

     Exhibit "D" -  Ordinance 1391 (additions to Article 14 of
                    the Fire Prevention Code)

     Exhibit "E" -  Existing Article 27 of the Zoning Ordinance

     Exhibit "F" -  Existing Article 14 of the Fire Prevention
                    Code

B.  The Family Tree Applications

     Also pending before the City Council is the application of
my employer's client, The Family Tree project, to convert 120 apart-
ments into condominiums. In connection with the application for
conversion, City Council action is required to approve a subdivision
map, approve a zoning change (from apartment zoning to PD zoning)
and grant a variance for parking and for two-hour fire wall protection.

C.  Subjective Statement

     Since 1973 I have been interested in the issue of apartment to
condominium conversion. I have met with builders, building officials,
attorneys, title company representatives, apartment owners, tenants,
realtors, legislators, and others to learn their viewpoints on the
subject. I did this because I had the education and the background
to intelligently evaluate the pros and cons of the issue. I did it
because I felt that the healthy preservation of our city's housing
stock is and will continue to be an important issue in our community,

embedding.qa_text: December 20, 1979 William A. Gissler, Mayor Office of the City Attorney City of Santa Clara 1500 Warburton Avenue Santa Clara, CA 95050 Dear Mr. Gissler: Thank you for your letter dated November 8, 1979, seeking advice pursuant to Government Code Section 83114(b) regarding the disqualification provision of the Political Reform Act. The facts, as I understand them, are as follows. You are Mayor of the City of Santa Clara and are presently employed by the engineering firm of Ruth & Going, Inc., a professional service organization that supplies architectural, planning, land survey, as well as engineering services to both public and private clients, including apartment complexes interested in converting to condominiums. Your present position with Ruth & Going is that of Senior Civil Service Engineer and your major function is to "interface with potential clients in a business development capacity." There have been four applications for condominium conversion before the City of Santa Clara - Vista Del Lago, Parkside Place, Family Tree Apartments and Lawrence Square West. Ruth & Going has been involved in some capacity with three of these four applicants. With respect to Vista Del Lago, Ruth & Going prepared the final map for condominium conversion; Ruth & Going was the civil engineering consultant on the Parkside Place conversion project; and Ruth & Going is under contract to Family Tree Apartments to provide engineering and other design services. Ruth & Going was not retained by Lawrence Square West to assist it with its condominium conversion application. With regard to Family Tree, the firm has represented Family Tree Apartments during all phases of its application for condominium conversion before the City. Your involvement to date with the Family Tree Project as an employee of Ruth & Going has consisted of consultations with William A. Gissler, Mayor December 20, 1979 Page Two both the project engineer and owners regarding both the economic and political viability of the projects. Pending before the City are two ordinances which would affect condominium conversion in the City of Santa Clara by easing some of the present requirements that have made condominium conversion difficult, if not prohibitively expensive. There are two questions that you have asked our office to consider. First, you have asked whether you may participate in proceedings regarding the proposed ordinances. Second, you have asked whether you must abstain from participating in decisions specifically relating to the Family Tree condominium conversion application. Answers to these questions require consideration of the disqualification provision of the Political Reform Act, Government Code Section 87100, which provides: No public official at any level of state or local government shall make, participate in making or in any way attempt to use his official position to influence a governmental decision in which he knows or has reason to know he has a financial interest. Government Code Section 87103 provides: An official has a financial interest in a decision within the meaning of Section 87100 if it is reasonably foreseeable that the decision will have a material financial
